metadata.title:
SHOULD THROW
OLD CHARTER


IN RIVER

pages.raw_text:
The sentiment In favor of revis-
Ing the city charter and making 1t
UP to date and practical from ey-
cry standpoint is constantly on the
increase. Men who have had to
work under the charter as public
officials are unanimous in advocat-
ing reform in this particular


City Treasurer Eggleston said
the proper thing to do with the
present charter was to throw it
into the river at the earliest OPP
portunity


"If the city charter was thrown
into the river and we had nothing
in the shape of a charter to work
under we would be better off than
at present." is the way Mr. Egg
gleston put it when discussing the
matter.


Former Mayor Byrne said he was
heartily in favor of revising the
charter. He intimated that at
present the mayor is blamed for
many things which was is unable
to prevent. DR.. Byrne said the
mayor should have the power to
remove appointive officials without
the consent of the city council, and
that the council should also have
the same right. This would tend
10 make appointive officials re-
sponsible to the elected officers and
would increase efficiency of the city
government. The doctor said a
mayor need not care so much who
was appointed to an Office provid-
ing he could remove the man at
any time. He said the city would
get pretty good service from any
holder of a public position if that
man knew he would be removed
when he was not filing the bill.


1t is generally conceded that un-
der present conditions the charter
Is useful only to politicians to
whack each other over the head. 1t
is used for club every time new
man assumes the Office of mayor.
The first thing he bumps up against
is the city charter and when he
gets through studying its various
provisions he has learned that he
is practically devoid OF power and
that every one else connected with
the city government is in just
about the same boat. 1t seemed
TO be the opinion of the trainers
of the present charter that the city
government would flow along as
peacefully as the St Joe river, anC
that the legislative executive
branches would never clash. 1t is
only when all departments are IL
complete harmony that anything
can be accomplished for the public
good.
